content:
Key Findings

  • Preston City Council spent £205.9M across 46,711 transactions from 2019 to 2025
  • Total spending increased by £32.0M (238.9%) from 2019 to 2025
  • The largest year-over-year increase was £25.2M (108.7%) from 2022 to 2023
  • The council transacted with 2,517 unique suppliers over the 7-year period
  • Spending peaked at £48.4M in 2023 before dropping by £3.957M in 2024

Preston's Spending Growth

Preston City Council's spending has seen significant growth over the past seven years, increasing by £32.0M (238.9%) from 2019 to 2025. The most notable surge occurred between 2022 and 2023, where spending jumped by £25.2M (108.7%), reaching a peak of £48.4M in 2023. This substantial increase suggests a period of heightened expenditure, potentially due to increased service demands or infrastructure projects.

The following year, 2024, saw a decrease of £3.957M (8.2%) in spending, bringing the total down to £44.4M. This reduction could indicate a return to more typical spending levels after the previous year's peak. The most recent data from 2025 shows a modest increase of £943K (2.1%), bringing spending to £45.4M.

Transparency and Data Coverage

Preston City Council has published 46,711 transactions over the seven-year period, providing a comprehensive view of its financial activities. This level of detail is crucial for transparency and allows residents to scrutinise how public funds are being used. The council's adherence to the Local Government Transparency Code ensures that this data is accessible to the public, fostering accountability and trust.

With 2,517 unique suppliers, the council's spending is spread across a wide range of service providers. This diversity can indicate a robust and competitive procurement process, ensuring that the council gets the best value for its expenditures. However, the sheer number of suppliers also highlights the complexity of managing such a vast network, which could present challenges in terms of oversight and efficiency.

Year-over-Year Spending Analysis

The year-over-year spending data reveals interesting trends in Preston City Council's financial management. The initial years from 2019 to 2021 saw relatively modest increases, with spending rising by £2.0M (14.7%) from 2019 to 2020 and £396K (2.6%) from 2020 to 2021. However, the period from 2021 to 2023 witnessed significant growth, with a £7.4M (47.1%) increase in 2022 and a £25.2M (108.7%) surge in 2023.

The table below summarises the year-over-year spending changes, providing a clear visual representation of the fluctuations in the council's expenditures.

Year Spending (£M) Change (£M) Change (%)
2019 13.4 - -
2020 15.4 +2.0 +14.7%
2021 15.8 +0.396 +2.6%
2022 23.2 +7.4 +47.1%
2023 48.4 +25.2 +108.7%
2024 44.4 -3.957 -8.2%
2025 45.4 +0.943 +2.1%

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Preston City Council about the specific projects or services that drove the significant spending increases in 2022 and 2023
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to learn more about the council's financial planning and expenditure priorities
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers received the largest contracts and for what purposes
  • Write to your local councillor to express any concerns or questions about the council's spending patterns and transparency
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and conduct your own analysis of Preston City Council's financial activities
This article is based on Preston City Council's published spending data covering 2019/20 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
